questions.text: What does CPU stand for?
Which device is used for input?
Which of these is a programming language?
What does RAM stand for?
Which one is an output device?
Which of these stores data permanently?
What does URL stand for?
Which of these is an operating system?
Which unit processes instructions?
Which of the following is a web browser?
Software is the physical part of a computer.
A keyboard is an input device.
RAM is non-volatile memory.
The internet and the web are the same thing.
An operating system manages hardware and software.
A printer is an input device.
ROM loses data when power is off.
CPU performs calculations and logic operations.
Email is a form of communication over the internet.
Hard disk is a temporary storage device.
